mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances following the trigger condition over a 10-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive return probability greater than 55% over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in post-shock recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with a restrictive policy lag create expectations of aggressive future monetary tightening, prompting portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in >60% of historical occurrences, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, which forces equity valuation multiples to contract and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below the threshold fails to predict negative returns, with forward return distribution centering above zero across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and total employment confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing default risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 5-day forward returns.
